Job poster

Message
About the job

Cloud Engineer

Remote, India 




NIKSUN is the recognized worldwide leader in making the Unknown Known, by using next-generation technology that revolutionizes the way networks and services are secured, protected, and managed. The company develops and deploys a complete range of award-winning forensics, compliance, security surveillance, and performance management solutions for applications ranging from core infrastructures to edge and branch environments.




NIKSUN is seeking an experienced cloud engineer, responsible for designing, implementing, and maintaining cloud-based infrastructure and services. This role is essential to ensuring the availability, performance, and scalability of our cloud-native services and platforms. The ideal candidate will bring hands-on experience in managing cloud environments and Kubernetes-based deployments in production.




Key responsibilities:




Operate, monitor, and optimize clusters for performance and health, including cluster upgrades, patching, and performance tuning.
Administer Kubernetes clusters including addition of new nodes, maintenance of existing nodes and infrastructure, management of taints and tolerations, etc
Ensure high availability, fault tolerance, and cost-efficiency across cloud-based systems.
Automate infrastructure tasks using Infrastructure as Code (IaC) tools such as Terraform, CloudFormation, or Ansible.
Build and support CI/CD pipelines via Jenkins to streamline application deployment.
Collaborate with development and DevOps teams to support application rollouts and incident management.
Monitor system health and respond to incidents with clear communication and effective troubleshooting.
Maintain detailed documentation for infrastructure, procedures, and runbooks.




Requirements: 




3+ years of experience in cloud engineering, DevOps, or related infrastructure roles.
Solid knowledge of Kubernetes operations, Helm, and container runtimes (Docker, containers).
Familiarity with CI/CD tools such as Jenkins and GitLab CI.
Strong understanding of Linux operating systems and their administration




Education:




Bachelor’s degree in computer science or computer engineering




The role involves working with customer engineering teams on a wide variety of cloud infrastructure projects, including migration, containerization, and transformation initiatives. Experience in regulated environments (HIPAA, PCI, ITAR, etc.) is beneficial.




The Cloud Engineer will be part of a dynamic team designing, automating, and deploying services for NIKSUN’s internal and external cloud environments. This is an ideal opportunity for candidates who enjoy a challenging and exciting work environment, where the problems they solve are constantly evolving.




Qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to age, race, creed, color, religion, sex, national origin, ancestry, marital status, affectional or sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, disability, nationality, or protected veteran status

… more

Requirements added by the job poster

• 3+ years of work experience with Linux

• Bachelor's Degree

• 3+ years of work experience with Kubernetes

• 3+ years of work experience with Docker Swarm

Job poster

Message
About the job

Position: Azure DevOps Engineer

Location: Work From Office – Hyderabad

Experience: 5+ Year

Hiring a DevOps Engineer with deep understanding of Kubernetes and Azure Infrastructure having 5+ years of experience in the respective technology.




InovarTech is a dynamic and innovative IT service and consulting company dedicated to providing cutting-edge solutions to our clients. With a focus on excellence, collaboration, and continuous improvement, we are committed to delivering superior results and fostering a positive and inclusive work environment.




We are seeking a skilled and collaborative Azure DevOps Engineer with 3–5 years of experience with deep understanding of Kubernetes and Azure infrastructure, and a passion for automation.




Primary Responsibilities:

Manage and maintain Kubernetes clusters, ensuring secure and scalable deployments.
Design and implement Helm charts for consistent and repeatable microservice deployments.
Architect and maintain CI/CD pipelines using GitLab Ci/CD (YAML), Azure DevOps, GitHub Actions.
Use Terraform to provision and manage cloud infrastructure in Microsoft Azure.
Collaborate directly with client-side IT/support teams including Raise and manage ITSM Tickets, communicate status clearly and professionally, occasionally work during Off-hours to accommodate client schedules.
Develop and maintain automation scripts in PowerShell or Bash for deployment, maintenance, and monitoring tasks.
Ensure monitoring, alerting, and logging are automated and reliable.
Understand microservices architecture and plan infrastructure needs accordingly.
Apply best practices in DevOps, infrastructure management, and security.




Required Skills & Qualifications

5+ years of experience in DevOps or Site Reliability Engineering roles.
Strong experience managing Kubernetes and container-based environments.
Proficiency with Helm for Kubernetes deployment automation.
Strong understanding of Docker.
Hands-on experience with CI/CD pipelines using GitLab, Azure DevOps, and GitHub Actions.
In-depth understanding of Azure Cloud Infrastructure and ARM templates, including networking, VMs, storage, and managed services.
Solid experience with Terraform for infrastructure provisioning.
Excellent communication skills—both verbal and written and should be comfortable interacting with clients and managing ITSM workflows.
Willingness to occasionally work outside normal business hours for critical deployments or client collaboration.
Proficiency in scripting with PowerShell or Bash for automation tasks.
Experience in Cloud Networking, Firewall, and Load Balancing
Experience creating scripts for monitoring and operational automation.
Basic knowledge of SQL and .NET applications is a strong advantage.
Experience with agile development process.
Strong experience in managing and configuring RBAC, managing identity and security best practices for cloud environments




Preferred Qualifications

Azure certifications (e.g., AZ-104, AZ-400).
Familiarity with monitoring tools like Prometheus, Grafana, or ELK Stack.
Understanding of DevSecOps and security automation in CI/CD.
Experience with ITSM tools (e.g., ServiceNow, Jira Service Desk).




What We Offer

Competitive salary and performance-based bonuses.
Comprehensive health and accident insurance plans.
Professional development opportunities.
Flexible work arrangements.
Fun and inclusive company culture.

Meet the hiring team

Sagar Dubey 

• 3rd+

Investment Advisor and Coach, Co-Founder ZeTheta Algorithms Private Limited

Job poster

Message
About the job

About the Company

ZeTheta Algorithms Private Limited is a FinTech start-up which has been recently set up and is developing innovative AI tools.




About the Role

The DevOps & Cloud Engineer role involves working on innovative financial technology projects that combine domain expertise with cutting-edge tools. This role offers hands-on experience building real-world systems through project-based work in a FinTech startup environment.




Assigned Projects

Interns will work on four core projects during the programme:

Design and deliver a production-grade, zero-downtime CI/CD pipeline
Design and deliver a complete multi-region disaster recovery
Design, build, and deliver a comprehensive Infrastructure Cost Optimisation and FinOps Framework
Design and deliver a production-grade, security-hardened Kubernetes platform




Key Responsibilities

Design and deliver a production-grade, zero-downtime CI/CD pipeline architecture for NovaPay Digital Bank, including eight canonical pipeline stages, blue-green and canary deployment strategies, and automated compliance gates mapped to RBI Master Direction and PCI-DSS v4. Design and deliver a complete multi-region disaster recovery architecture for PaySecure Gateway. Design, build, and deliver a comprehensive Infrastructure Cost Optimisation and FinOps Framework for LendFlow Technologies, achieving a minimum 33% cost reduction without degrading performance or breaching compliance boundaries. Design and deliver a production-grade, security-hardened Kubernetes platform for FinServ Digital, implementing zero-trust network policies, least-privilege RBAC, HashiCorp Vault, Falco runtime detection, and full PCI-DSS Level 1 compliance.




Eligibility

Students enrolled in High School, Undergraduate, Postgraduate, or MBA programs from ANY academic discipline, or fresh graduates from any field. No prior work experience required. Self-motivated learners with an interest in FinTech and financial markets.




Qualifications

Familiarity with cloud platforms (AWS, GCP, or Azure) and containerisation (Docker, Kubernetes). Understanding of CI/CD pipelines, infrastructure-as-code, and DevSecOps principles. Knowledge of compliance frameworks such as PCI-DSS, RBI IT Framework, or SOC 2. Interest in FinOps, disaster recovery architecture, and platform reliability engineering.




What You Will Learn

Practical experience building financial technology applications. Understanding of financial markets, trading, and risk management concepts. Hands-on exposure to cutting-edge tools and technologies relevant to the role. Portfolio-worthy projects demonstrating technical and domain expertise. Collaboration skills working in a startup environment.




Internship Details

Duration: Self-paced. Options of 1, 2, 3, 4, or 6 months.

Type: Remote, Project-based.

Compensation: Unpaid. Certificate of Project Work Experience (Internship / Externship).

Location: Work from anywhere.




Application Process

Join us through online enrolment on https://www.zetheta.com/linkedin-apply/ and simply submit your updated CV. We do not conduct any interview or assessment. All motivated early career talent are welcome to take up the role.
